sentences.sentence: Most people today think of chocolate as something sweet to eat or drink that can be easily found in stores around the world.
sentences.translation: 如今，大多数人认为巧克力是一种可以在世界各地商店轻易买到的甜食或甜饮。

structure.text: think of chocolate as something sweet to eat or drink
that can be easily found in stores around the world

difficult_points: think of A as B 表示把A看作B，A是chocolate，B是something sweet to eat or drink。
to eat or drink 是不定式作后置定语，修饰 something sweet。
that can be easily found... 是定语从句，修饰 something sweet to eat or drink，被动语态强调易于购得。

sentences.sentence: It might surprise you that chocolate was once highly treasured.
sentences.translation: 巧克力曾经备受珍视，这一点可能会让你感到意外。

structure.text: that chocolate was once highly treasured

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正的主语是后面 that 引导的从句。
highly treasured 是被动语态，表示曾经备受珍视。

sentences.sentence: The tasty secret of the cacao (Kah Kow) tree was discovered 2,000 years ago in the tropical rainforests of the Americas.
sentences.translation: 可可（Kah Kow）树的美味秘密，是在两千年前于美洲的热带雨林中被发现的。

structure.text: The tasty secret of the cacao (Kah Kow) tree
in the tropical rainforests of the Americas

difficult_points: was discovered 是被动语态，强调秘密“被发现”而非主动发现者。
2,000 years ago 和 in the tropical rainforests of the Americas 分别说明发现的时间和地点。

sentences.sentence: The story of how chocolate grew from a local Mesoamerican beverage into a global sweet encompasses many cultures and continents.
sentences.translation: 巧克力如何从中美洲当地的一种饮品发展成全球流行甜食的故事，涉及众多文化与大陆。

structure.text: The story of how chocolate grew from a local Mesoamerican beverage into a global sweet
how chocolate grew from a local Mesoamerican beverage into a global sweet

difficult_points: how 引导的从句作 story of 的宾语，说明故事的具体内容。
from A into B 表示“从A发展/转变为B”。
encompasses 意为“涵盖、涉及”，宾语是 many cultures and continents。

sentences.sentence: Historians believe the Maya people of Central America first learned to farm cacao plants around two thousand years ago.
sentences.translation: 历史学家认为，中美洲的玛雅人大约在两千年前最早学会了种植可可。

structure.text: the Maya people of Central America first learned to farm cacao plants around two thousand years ago
the Maya people of Central America
first learned to farm cacao plants

difficult_points: believe 后省略了 that，其后接宾语从句。
learn to do 表示“学会做某事”，此处指学会种植可可。

sentences.sentence: The Maya took cacao trees from the rainforests and grew them in their gardens.
sentences.translation: 玛雅人把可可树从雨林中移栽出来，种在自家的园子里。

structure.text: took cacao trees from the rainforests
and grew them in their gardens

difficult_points: took ... and grew ... 是并列谓语，共用主语 The Maya。
them 指代前面的 cacao trees。

sentences.sentence: They cooked cacao seeds, the crushed them into a soft paste.
sentences.translation: 他们把可可豆煮熟，然后将其捣成软膏状的糊。

structure.text: the crushed them into a soft paste

difficult_points: 原文 the crushed 为 then crushed 的笔误，指“然后将其捣碎”。
them 指代前面的 cacao seeds。

sentences.sentence: They mixed the paste with water and flavorful spices to make an unsweetened chocolate drink.
sentences.translation: 他们把糊状物与水和香料混合，制成一种未加糖的巧克力饮品。

structure.text: mixed the paste with water and flavorful spices
to make an unsweetened chocolate drink

difficult_points: mix A with B 表示“把A和B混合”。
to make... 是不定式作目的状语，说明混合的目的。

sentences.sentence: The Maya poured the chocolate drink back and forth between two containers so that the liquid would have a layer of bubbles or foam.
sentences.translation: 玛雅人把巧克力饮品在两个容器之间来回倾倒，好让液体表面形成一层泡沫。

structure.text: poured the chocolate drink back and forth between two containers
so that the liquid would have a layer of bubbles or foam

difficult_points: back and forth between... 表示“在……之间来回”。
so that 引导结果/目的状语从句，说明来回倾倒的目的。

sentences.sentence: Cacao and chocolate were an important part of Maya culture.
sentences.translation: 可可与巧克力是玛雅文化中重要的一部分。

structure.text: an important part of Maya culture

difficult_points: Cacao and chocolate 是并列主语，谓语用复数 were。
an important part of 表示“……的重要组成部分”。

sentences.sentence: There are often images of cacao plants on Maya buildings and art objects.
sentences.translation: 玛雅人的建筑和艺术品上常常绘有可可植物的图像。

structure.text: There are often images of cacao plants
on Maya buildings and art objects

difficult_points: There are 是存在句结构，真正的信息中心是 images of cacao plants。
on Maya buildings and art objects 说明图像出现的位置。

sentences.sentence: Ruling families drank chocolate at special ceremonies.
sentences.translation: 王室家族会在特殊的仪式上饮用巧克力。
difficult_points: Ruling families 指统治阶层或王室家族。
at special ceremonies 说明饮用巧克力的场合。

sentences.sentence: And, even poorer members of society could enjoy the drink once in a while.
sentences.translation: 而且，即便是社会中较贫穷的成员，也能偶尔享用这种饮品。
difficult_points: even 强调“即便是……也”，突出贫穷成员也能享用巧克力。
once in a while 表示“偶尔”。

sentences.sentence: Historians believe that cacao seeds were also used in marriage ceremonies as a sign of the union between a husband and a wife.
sentences.translation: 历史学家认为，可可豆也曾被用于婚礼仪式，象征夫妻的结合。

structure.text: that cacao seeds were also used in marriage ceremonies as a sign of the union between a husband and a wife
were also used in marriage ceremonies
as a sign of the union between a husband and a wife

difficult_points: were used 是被动语态，强调可可豆“被使用”于婚礼。
as a sign of 表示“作为……的象征”。

sentences.sentence: The Aztec culture in current-day Mexico also prized chocolate.
sentences.translation: 如今墨西哥一带的阿兹特克文化同样十分珍视巧克力。

structure.text: The Aztec culture in current-day Mexico

difficult_points: current-day Mexico 指“如今的墨西哥地区”，说明阿兹特克文化的地理位置。
prize 在此作动词，意为“珍视”。

sentences.sentence: But, cacao plants could not grow in the area where the Aztecs lived.
sentences.translation: 但可可植物却无法在阿兹特克人居住的地区生长。

structure.text: could not grow in the area

difficult_points: where 引导定语从句，修饰 the area，说明阿兹特克人生活的地区。
could not grow 使用情态动词 could 的否定式，表示“不能够生长”。

sentences.sentence: So, they traded to get cacao.
sentences.translation: 因此，他们通过贸易来获取可可。
difficult_points: to get cacao 是不定式作目的状语，说明贸易的目的。
So 在句首表示“因此”，连接前后因果关系。

sentences.sentence: They even used cacao seeds as a form of money to pay taxes.
sentences.translation: 他们甚至把可可豆当作一种货币来缴税。

structure.text: even used cacao seeds as a form of money

difficult_points: use A as B 表示“把A当作B使用”。
to pay taxes 是不定式作目的状语。

expressions.text: use ... as a form of money

sentences.sentence: Chocolate also played a special role in both Maya and Aztec royal and religious events.
sentences.translation: 巧克力在玛雅和阿兹特克的王室及宗教活动中也扮演着特殊的角色。

structure.text: in both Maya and Aztec royal and religious events

difficult_points: play a role in 表示“在……中发挥作用”。
both Maya and Aztec 并列修饰 royal and religious events，指两种文化的相关活动。

sentences.sentence: Priests presented cacao seeds and offerings to the gods and served chocolate drinks during sacred ceremonies.
sentences.translation: 祭司们向神灵献上可可豆和祭品，并在神圣的仪式上奉上巧克力饮品。

structure.text: presented cacao seeds and offerings to the gods
and served chocolate drinks during sacred ceremonies

difficult_points: presented ... and served ... 是并列谓语，共用主语 Priests。
present A to B 表示“把A献给B”。

sentences.sentence: Only the very wealthy in Aztec societies could afford to drink chocolate because cacao was so valuable.
sentences.translation: 只有阿兹特克社会中的极富之人才能负担得起饮用巧克力，因为可可十分珍贵。

structure.text: Only the very wealthy in Aztec societies

difficult_points: the very wealthy 是“the + 形容词”结构，泛指“极富有的人”。
afford to do 表示“负担得起做某事”。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释只有富人能喝巧克力的原因。

sentences.sentence: The Aztec ruler Montezuma was believed to drink fifty cups of chocolate every day.
sentences.translation: 据信，阿兹特克统治者蒙特祖玛每天要喝五十杯巧克力。

structure.text: was believed to drink fifty cups of chocolate every day
to drink fifty cups of chocolate every day

difficult_points: was believed to do 是“sb be believed to do”结构，表示“据信……”。
fifty cups of chocolate every day 用具体数字夸张地说明蒙特祖玛饮用巧克力之多。

sentences.sentence: Some experts believe the word for chocolate came from the Aztec word "xocolatl" which in the Nahuatl language means "bitter water."
sentences.translation: 一些专家认为，“chocolate”一词源自阿兹特克语中的“xocolatl”，在纳瓦特尔语中意为“苦水”。

structure.text: the word for chocolate came from the Aztec word "xocolatl"
which in the Nahuatl language means "bitter water."

difficult_points: believe 后省略了 that，引出宾语从句。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 "xocolatl"，补充说明其含义。
in the Nahuatl language 插在 which 和 means 之间，说明该词义所属的语言。

sentences.sentence: Others believe the word "chocolate" was created by combining Mayan and Nahuatl words.
sentences.translation: 另一些人则认为，“chocolate”一词是由玛雅语和纳瓦特尔语的词汇组合而成的。

structure.text: the word "chocolate" was created by combining Mayan and Nahuatl words
by combining Mayan and Nahuatl words

difficult_points: believe 后省略 that，接宾语从句。
was created by doing 表示“通过……的方式产生的”，by 后接动名词。

sentences.sentence: The explorer Christopher Columbus brought cacao seeds to Spain after his trip to Central America in 1502.
sentences.translation: 探险家克里斯托弗·哥伦布在1502年前往中美洲之后，把可可豆带回了西班牙。

structure.text: after his trip to Central America in 1502

difficult_points: bring A to B 表示“把A带到B”。
after his trip to... 说明带回可可豆发生在这次中美洲之行之后。

sentences.sentence: But it was the Spanish explorer Hernando Cortes who understood that chocolate could be a valuable investment.
sentences.translation: 但真正意识到巧克力可能是一项宝贵投资的，是西班牙探险家埃尔南·科尔特斯。

structure.text: it was the Spanish explorer Hernando Cortes
who understood that chocolate could be a valuable investment
that chocolate could be a valuable investment

difficult_points: it was ... who ... 是强调句结构，强调的是 the Spanish explorer Hernando Cortes。
who 引导的从句中又嵌套 that 引导的宾语从句，作 understood 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: In 1519, Cortes arrived in current-day Mexico.
sentences.translation: 1519年，科尔特斯抵达了如今的墨西哥地区。
difficult_points: In 1519 提前至句首，强调事件发生的时间。
current-day Mexico 指“如今的墨西哥地区”，即当时阿兹特克帝国所在地。

sentences.sentence: He believed the chocolate drink would become popular with Spaniards.
sentences.translation: 他相信巧克力饮品会在西班牙人中流行起来。

structure.text: the chocolate drink would become popular with Spaniards

difficult_points: believed 后省略 that，引出宾语从句。
become popular with 表示“受到……的欢迎、在……中流行”。

sentences.sentence: After the Spanish soldiers defeated the Aztec empire, they were able to seize the supplies of cacao and send them home.
sentences.translation: 西班牙士兵击败阿兹特克帝国之后，得以夺取可可储备并将其运回本国。

structure.text: After the Spanish soldiers defeated the Aztec empire
were able to seize the supplies of cacao

difficult_points: After 引导时间状语从句，说明夺取可可发生在击败阿兹特克帝国之后。
seize ... and send ... 是并列谓语，共用主语 they。
them 指代前面的 the supplies of cacao。

sentences.sentence: Spain later began planting cacao in its colonies in the Americans in order to satisfy the large demand for chocolate.
sentences.translation: 此后，西班牙开始在其美洲殖民地种植可可，以满足人们对巧克力的巨大需求。

structure.text: later began planting cacao in its colonies in the Americans
planting cacao in its colonies in the Americans
in order to satisfy the large demand for chocolate

difficult_points: began doing 表示“开始做某事”，动名词作宾语。
in order to 引导目的状语，说明西班牙种植可可的目的。
the Americans 为原文用词，此处实际指“美洲（the Americas）”这一地域。

sentences.sentence: The wealthy people of Spain first enjoyed a sweetened version of chocolate drink.
sentences.translation: 西班牙的富人最先享用了加糖版本的巧克力饮品。

structure.text: a sweetened version of chocolate drink

difficult_points: a sweetened version of 指“加糖后的版本”，与之前玛雅、阿兹特克人饮用的无糖巧克力形成对比。
first 强调西班牙富人是最早享用这种加糖版本的群体。

sentences.sentence: Later, the popularity of the drink spread throughout Europe.
sentences.translation: 后来，这种饮品的流行风潮传遍了整个欧洲。
difficult_points: spread throughout 表示“遍及、传遍”。
Later 承接上一句，表示时间上的先后顺序。

sentences.sentence: The English, Dutch and French began to plant cacao trees in their own colonies.
sentences.translation: 英国人、荷兰人和法国人也开始在各自的殖民地种植可可树。

structure.text: began to plant cacao trees in their own colonies
to plant cacao trees in their own colonies

difficult_points: The English, Dutch and French 是“the + 形容词”表示三个民族的人，谓语用复数。
began to do 表示“开始做某事”。

sentences.sentence: Chocolate remained a drink that only wealthy people could afford to drink until the eighteenth century.
sentences.translation: 直到18世纪，巧克力都还是一种只有富人才喝得起的饮品。

structure.text: that only wealthy people could afford to drink

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 a drink，说明这种饮品只有富人能负担得起。
until the eighteenth century 说明这种状况持续的时间终点。

sentences.sentence: During the period known as the Industrial Revolution, new technologies helped make chocolate less costly to produce.
sentences.translation: 在被称为工业革命的时期，新技术使得生产巧克力的成本降低。

structure.text: helped make chocolate less costly to produce

difficult_points: known as... 是过去分词短语，修饰 the period，说明这一时期即工业革命。
help do sth 表示“有助于做某事”，此处省略了动词不定式符号 to。
less costly to produce 中 to produce 说明“costly”所指的方面，即生产成本。

sentences.sentence: Farmers grow cacao trees in many countries in Africa, Central and South America.
sentences.translation: 农民们在非洲、中美洲和南美洲的许多国家种植可可树。

structure.text: in many countries in Africa, Central and South America

difficult_points: in many countries in... 列举可可种植的主要地区。
Central and South America 与 Africa 并列，共同构成可可种植的三大地区。

sentences.sentence: The trees grow in the shady areas of the rainforests near the Earth's equator.
sentences.translation: 这些树木生长在靠近赤道的雨林阴凉地带。

structure.text: grow in the shady areas of the rainforests

difficult_points: near the Earth's equator 修饰 the rainforests，说明雨林的地理位置。
the shady areas of 强调这些树木偏好阴凉的生长环境。

sentences.sentence: But these trees can be difficult to grow.
sentences.translation: 但这些树木可能很难种植。
difficult_points: difficult to grow 中不定式 to grow 说明“difficult”所指向的动作，即种植这件事。
But 承接前句，形成转折，说明可可树虽分布广泛却不易栽培。

sentences.sentence: They require an exact amount of water, warmth, soil and protection.
sentences.translation: 它们需要恰到好处的水分、温度、土壤条件和保护。

structure.text: an exact amount of water, warmth, soil and protection

difficult_points: an exact amount of 修饰并列的四个名词，表示“恰好的量”。
water, warmth, soil and protection 四个并列名词说明可可树生长所需的具体条件。

sentences.sentence: After about five years, cacao trees start producing large fruits called pods, which grow near the trunk of the tree.
sentences.translation: 大约五年后，可可树开始结出被称为“荚果”的大型果实，这些果实生长在树干附近。

structure.text: which grow near the trunk of the tree

difficult_points: start doing 表示“开始做某事”。
called pods 是过去分词短语，修饰 large fruits，说明这种果实的名称。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明这些果实的生长位置。

sentences.sentence: The seeds inside the pods are harvested to make chocolate.
sentences.translation: 荚果内的种子会被采收下来用于制作巧克力。
difficult_points: are harvested 是被动语态，强调种子“被采收”。
to make chocolate 是不定式作目的状语。

sentences.sentence: There are several kinds of cacao trees.
sentences.translation: 可可树有好几个品种。

structure.text: There are several kinds of cacao trees

difficult_points: There are 存在句结构，引出可可树的品种数量。
several kinds of 表示“几种”，为下文具体介绍各品种做铺垫。

sentences.sentence: Most of the world's chocolate is made from the seed of the forastero tree.
sentences.translation: 世界上大部分巧克力都是用福拉斯特罗可可树的种子制成的。

structure.text: is made from the seed of the forastero tree

difficult_points: be made from 表示“由……制成”，强调原材料。
Most of 表示“大部分”，暗示福拉斯特罗并非唯一的可可品种。

sentences.sentence: But farmers can also grow criollo or trinitario cacao plants.
sentences.translation: 但农民也可以种植克里奥罗或特立尼达可可品种。

structure.text: can also grow criollo or trinitario cacao plants

difficult_points: criollo 和 trinitario 是另外两种可可品种的名称，与前一句的 forastero 并列构成三大主要品种。
also 呼应上一句，表示农民除 forastero 外还可种植其他品种。

sentences.sentence: Cacao trees grown on farms are much more easily threatened by diseases and insects than wild trees.
sentences.translation: 种植在农场里的可可树，比野生可可树更容易受到病害和虫害的威胁。

structure.text: are much more easily threatened by diseases and insects

difficult_points: grown on farms 是过去分词短语，修饰 Cacao trees，指“种植在农场里的”可可树。
are threatened by 是被动语态，more easily 加强比较，than wild trees 构成比较结构。

sentences.sentence: Growing cacao is very hard work for farmers.
sentences.translation: 对农民来说，种植可可是件非常辛苦的工作。
difficult_points: Growing cacao 是动名词短语作主语，指“种植可可”这件事。
very hard work 强调种植可可对农民而言劳动强度很大。

sentences.sentence: They sell their harvest on a futures market.
sentences.translation: 他们要在期货市场上出售自己的收成。
difficult_points: a futures market 指“期货市场”，说明可可收成的交易方式。
their harvest 指代前面提到的农民所种植、采收的可可作物。

sentences.sentence: This means that economic conditions beyond their control can affect the amount of money they will earn.
sentences.translation: 这意味着，他们无法掌控的经济状况会影响到他们最终能挣到的钱数。

structure.text: that economic conditions beyond their control can affect the amount of money they will earn

difficult_points: This 指代前一句“在期货市场出售收成”这一做法。
beyond their control 修饰 economic conditions，说明这些经济状况超出了农民的掌控。
they will earn 前省略了关系代词 that/which，修饰 the amount of money。

sentences.sentence: Today, chocolate industry officials, activists, and scientists are working with farmers.
sentences.translation: 如今，巧克力行业的官员、活动人士和科学家们正在与农民合作。

structure.text: chocolate industry officials, activists, and scientists

difficult_points: officials, activists, and scientists 是并列主语，谓语用复数 are working。
Today 表示这是当下正在进行的合作，与前文的历史叙述形成时间上的转换。

sentences.sentence: They are trying to make sure that cacao can be grown in a way that is fair to the timers and safe for the environment.
sentences.translation: 他们正努力确保可可的种植方式，既能公平对待农民（原文误作 timers），又不损害环境。

structure.text: that cacao can be grown in a way
that is fair to the timers and safe for the environment

difficult_points: try to do 表示“努力做某事”。
make sure that... 引出确保的具体内容。
that is fair... 引导定语从句，修饰 a way，与前一个引导宾语从句的 that 层级不同。
timers 为原文用词，根据语境应为 farmers（农民）的笔误。

sentences.sentence: To become chocolate, cacao seeds go through a long production process in a factory.
sentences.translation: 为了变成巧克力，可可豆要在工厂里经历一道漫长的生产过程。

structure.text: go through a long production process in a factory

difficult_points: To become chocolate 是不定式作目的状语，置于句首。
go through 表示“经历、经过”。

sentences.sentence: Workers must sort, clean and cook the seeds.
sentences.translation: 工人们必须对可可豆进行分拣、清洗和蒸煮。

structure.text: must sort, clean and cook the seeds

difficult_points: sort, clean and cook 是三个并列动词，共用宾语 the seeds。
must 表示这是生产流程中的必要步骤。

sentences.sentence: Then they break off the covering of the seeds so that only the inside fruit, or nibs, remain.
sentences.translation: 接着他们剥去豆子的外皮，只留下里面的果肉，也就是可可碎粒。

structure.text: break off the covering of the seeds
so that only the inside fruit, or nibs, remain

difficult_points: break off 表示“剥离、去掉”。
so that 引导结果状语从句，说明剥皮的结果。
or nibs 是对 the inside fruit 的补充说明，即行业术语“可可碎粒”。

sentences.sentence: Workers crush the nibs into a soft substance called chocolate liquor.
sentences.translation: 工人们把可可碎粒磨碎，制成一种被称为“可可液块”的软质物质。

structure.text: crush the nibs into a soft substance

difficult_points: crush ... into ... 表示“把……捣碎成……”。
called chocolate liquor 是过去分词短语，修饰 a soft substance，说明其名称。

sentences.sentence: This gets separated into cocoa solids and fat called cocoa butter.
sentences.translation: 这种物质随后被分离成可可固形物和被称为“可可脂”的脂肪。

structure.text: gets separated into cocoa solids and fat

difficult_points: gets separated 是“get + 过去分词”构成的被动结构，表示“被分离”。
called cocoa butter 是过去分词短语，修饰 fat，说明其名称。

sentences.sentence: Chocolate makers have their own special recipes in which they combine chocolate liquor with exact amounts of sugar, milk and cocoa fat.
sentences.translation: 巧克力制造商都有各自独特的配方，他们按照精确的比例，把可可液块与糖、牛奶及可可脂混合。

structure.text: in which they combine chocolate liquor with exact amounts of sugar, milk and cocoa fat

difficult_points: in which 引导定语从句，修饰 recipes，相当于 in the recipes they combine...。
combine A with B 表示“把A与B混合”。

sentences.sentence: They finely crush this "crumb" mixture in order to make it smooth.
sentences.translation: 他们将这种“碎屑状”混合物细细研磨，使其变得顺滑。

structure.text: in order to make it smooth

difficult_points: in order to 引导目的状语，说明研磨的目的。
make it smooth 是“make + 宾语 + 形容词”结构，表示“使其变得顺滑”。

sentences.sentence: The mixture then goes through two more processes before it is shaped into a mold form.
sentences.translation: 随后，这种混合物还要再经历两道工序，才会被塑形成模具的形状。

structure.text: then goes through two more processes
before it is shaped into a mold form

difficult_points: before 引导时间状语从句，说明这两道工序发生在塑形之前。
is shaped into 是被动语态，表示“被塑造成”。

sentences.sentence: Chocolate making is big business.
sentences.translation: 巧克力制造是一门大生意。
difficult_points: Chocolate making 是动名词短语作主语。
big business 表示巧克力制造已发展成一项规模巨大的产业。

sentences.sentence: The market value of the yearly cacao crop around the world is more than five billion dollars.
sentences.translation: 全球每年可可产量的市场价值超过五十亿美元。

structure.text: The market value of the yearly cacao crop around the world
is more than five billion dollars

difficult_points: of the yearly cacao crop around the world 修饰 market value，说明是全球每年可可产量的市场价值。
more than 表示“超过”，强调市场价值之高。

sentences.sentence: Chocolate is especially popular in Europe and the United States.
sentences.translation: 巧克力在欧洲和美国尤其受欢迎。

structure.text: in Europe and the United States

difficult_points: especially popular in 表示“在……尤其受欢迎”。
Europe and the United States 举例说明巧克力最受欢迎的两大区域。

sentences.sentence: For example, in 2005, the United States bought 1.4 billion dollars worth of cocoa products.
sentences.translation: 例如，2005年美国购买了价值14亿美元的可可产品。

structure.text: bought 1.4 billion dollars worth of cocoa products

difficult_points: ... dollars worth of ... 表示“价值……美元的……”。
For example 承接上一句，用具体数据佐证巧克力在美国的受欢迎程度。

sentences.sentence: Each year, Americans eat an average of more than five kilograms of chocolate per person.
sentences.translation: 美国人平均每年每人要吃掉超过五公斤的巧克力。

structure.text: eat an average of more than five kilograms of chocolate per person

difficult_points: an average of 表示“平均……”，per person 说明是按人均计算。
Each year 强调这是逐年持续的消费情况，而非某一年的特例。

sentences.sentence: Speciality shops that sell costly chocolates are also very popular.
sentences.translation: 出售昂贵巧克力的特色商店也十分受欢迎。
difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 Speciality shops，说明这些商店的经营特点。
also 呼应前文巧克力本身的流行，表示专卖店同样广受欢迎。

sentences.sentence: Many offer chocolate lovers the chance to taste chocolates grown in different areas of the world.
sentences.translation: 许多这样的商店会为巧克力爱好者提供机会，品尝产自世界不同地区的巧克力。

structure.text: to taste chocolates grown in different areas of the world
grown in different areas of the world

difficult_points: offer sb sth 是双宾语结构，sb 是 chocolate lovers，sth 是 the chance。
to taste... 是不定式修饰 the chance，说明机会的具体内容。
grown in different areas of the world 是过去分词短语，修饰 chocolates，说明其产地。

expressions.text: offer sb the chance to do sth
expressions.meaning_zh: 给某人提供做某事的机会
